senses.definition: A chemical name referring to a crystalline acid formed by heating tartaric acid, known as pyrotritaric acid.
examples: Pyrotritaric acid is sometimes discussed in advanced organic chemistry literature.
The synthesis involved converting tartaric acid to pyrotritaric acid under controlled conditions.

senses.definition: Relating to or describing pyrotritaric compounds or processes involving pyrotritaric acid.
examples: The pyrotritartric synthesis pathway was explored in the paper.
Researchers noted pyrotritartric properties during the reaction.

senses.definition: Relating to pyrouric acids or pyrouric compounds, a class of chemical substances.
examples: The pyrouric compound exhibited unusual reactivity.
Pyrouric derivatives were analyzed in the study.

senses.definition: A chemical salt or ester containing vanadium and oxygen, derived from vanadic acid and used in chemistry.
examples: The solution contained pyrovanadate ions.
Researchers referenced pyrovanadate in catalysis discussions.

senses.definition: Relating to or containing pyrovanadate species or vanadium in a pyro form.
examples: Pyrovanadic compounds were studied for their redox properties.
The pyrovanadic solution showed characteristic absorption bands.

senses.definition: A historical or rare term possibly referring to a mineral or pigment in pyroxene-related contexts.
examples: The mineral catalog lists pyroxantin among related species.

senses.definition: A common rock-forming mineral that is usually green, brown, or black and forms in silicate rocks.
examples: Olivine and pyroxene are typical in mafic rocks.
The mineral composition included pyroxene.

senses.definition: The plural form of pyroxene; a group of minerals that are common in rocks.
examples: Pyroxenes vary in composition across different rocks.
Geologists identify pyroxenes under polarized light.

senses.definition: Relating to pyroxene or containing pyroxene.
examples: A pyroxenic rock sample was collected for analysis.
The crystals showed pyroxenic characteristics.

senses.definition: A dark, heavy igneous rock consisting mainly of pyroxene minerals.
examples: The quarry yielded several samples of pyroxenite.
Pyroxenite is often richer in pyroxene than other rocks.

senses.definition: The plural form of pyroxenite; rocks composed mainly of pyroxene minerals.
examples: Several pyroxenites were examined in the field.
Petrologists study pyroxenites to understand mantle processes.

senses.definition: Relating to pyroxenite or containing pyroxene in its composition.
examples: The pyroxenitic rock displayed dense mineral bands.
Chemical analyses confirmed pyroxenitic characteristics.

senses.definition: A mineral that is related to pyroxenes and forms elongated crystals; part of the pyroxenoid group.
examples: The specimen is classified as a pyroxenoid.
Pyroxenoid minerals are found in metamorphic rocks.

senses.definition: A group of minerals chemically related to pyroxenes, including pyroxenoids.
examples: The suite includes several pyroxenoids.
Pyroxenoids form under specific metamorphic conditions.

senses.definition: A mineral in the pyroxene group containing manganese; a member of the manganiferous pyroxenes.
examples: The rock includes traces of pyroxmangite.
Manganese-rich pyroxenes like pyroxmangite were identified.

senses.definition: A hypothetical or historical pyroxene-related mineral or compound; the term may appear in mineralogical literature.
examples: The sample was compared to known pyroxonium standards.

senses.definition: A historical or rare term in chemistry or mineralogy possibly denoting a pyroxyl compound or related substance.
examples: References to pyroxylenes and related pyroxyle compounds appear in old catalogs.

senses.definition: A chemical substance related to xylene in the context of pyroxyl chemistry; may appear in older chemical literature.
examples: Pyroxylene was discussed in the historical text on organic reagents.

senses.definition: Plural form of pyroxyle; refers to related chemical substances in older literature.
examples: Several pyroxyles were cataloged in the 19th century.

senses.definition: Relating to or derived from pyroxyl or substances formed by pyrolysis.
examples: The sample showed pyroxylic decomposition products.
Pyroxylic reactions were described in the early literature.

senses.definition: A viscous liquid obtained from cellulose (cellulose nitrate) used historically as a varnish or film-forming agent.
examples: Historical wood finishes used pyroxylin as a binder.
Pyroxylin solutions were common in early plastics research.

senses.definition: A solution or derivative related to pyroxylin (cellulose nitrate) used in finishes or coatings.
examples: The manufacturer produced a pyroxyline varnish.

senses.definition: Plural of pyroxyline; substances related to pyroxyline chemistry.
examples: Several pyroxyline formulations were tested.

senses.definition: Plural form of pyroxylin; viscous cellulose nitrate derivatives used in coatings or films.
examples: Old varnish catalogs list several pyroxylins.

senses.definition: In mythology, Pyrrha is a figure known for surviving the great flood and repopulating the earth; also used as a given name or in literary/historical contexts.
examples: Pyrrha appears in classical myths as the daughter of Epimetheus and Pandora.
The character Pyrrha is referenced in the novel as a legendary figure.
